title: mentha pascua
intro: Mentha pulegium, commonly known as Mentha pascua, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family. It is native to Europe and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of essential oils, including pulegone and menthone. The mentha pascua plant has been found to possess antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can provide several health benefits, including relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, mentha pascua is often used as a tincture, tea, or essential oil, and can be used to treat various health conditions, such as colds, coughs, and respiratory issues.
